summary: Matched the Tournament Peach ground/stage module by replacing `asm/melee/gr/grtpeach.s` with `src/melee/gr/grtpeach.c`, wiring the object list to compile the new C file, adding the public `grtpeach.h` StageData export, and making small header/tooling adjustments needed for matching and context generation. The PR report shows `src/melee/gr/grtpeach.c` at 100.00% with all listed functions matched.
what_changed: Added a 202-line C implementation `src/melee/gr/grtpeach.c` containing the `grTPeach_802228B4` through `grTPeach_80222B90` functions, the `grTPe_803E90F0` StageCallbacks table, and the `grTPe_803E914C` StageData table.
Added `src/melee/gr/grtpeach.h` with an extern declaration for `StageData grTPe_803E914C`.
Expanded `src/melee/gr/forward.h` to include `<baselib/forward.h>` and to define a full `Ground_GObj` layout under `M2CTX`, while typedefing `Ground_GObj` to `HSD_GObj` for normal builds.
Added explicit field-offset comments to `StageCallbacks` in `src/melee/gr/types.h` without changing its member order or types.
Changed `tools/convert_data.py`'s `input_glob` from `**/it/items/itsword.s` to `**/grtpeach.s`, apparently to target this module's data conversion workflow.
smart_moves: Kept the matched C close to the assembly structure: static address-named functions, address comments, direct callback table reconstruction, and simple wrappers for empty/constant-return functions.

Represented raw data constants as meaningful C initializers where evidence allowed, such as `"/GrTPe.dat"`, callback tables, `StageData`, and flag expressions like `(1 << 0) | (1 << 1) | (1 << 2) | (1 << 4) | (1 << 5)`.

Used existing ground/stage helpers and macros rather than open-coded offsets in the C implementation: `GET_GROUND`, `GET_JOBJ`, `Ground_801C14D0`, `GObj_SetupGXLink`, `grAnime_801C8138`, `Ground_801C2ED0`, and `Ground_801C2FE0`.
Introduced `Ground_GObj` as an M2CTX-only concrete struct so context generation can see field layout while normal compilation continues using the existing `HSD_GObj` typedef.
Preserved a matching-sensitive local declaration/order issue with an explicit note in `grTPeach_80222958`: `@todo Can't move below @c callbacks.`
decomp_lessons: Stage modules often match cleanly by first reconstructing `StageCallbacks` and `StageData`; their data tables reveal the init callbacks, per-map callbacks, file path string, and function pointer order.
For ground object code, introducing a context-only typedef/struct can unblock m2ctx and type propagation without forcing a broad runtime type migration.

Do not assume empty assembly functions are mistakes; in this module several functions intentionally compile to `blr` or constant `return false`/`return true` stubs and are part of the callback table ABI.
When matching small stage setup functions, bitfield writes can become readable `stage_info` flag assignments if the surrounding struct fields are already known.
Local declaration placement can affect matching even when semantically equivalent; keep comments documenting such fragile ordering constraints for future cleanup.
naming_conventions: Functions retained address-based names with the module prefix: `grTPeach_802228B4`, `grTPeach_80222958`, `grTPeach_80222B90`.
Data symbols use the shorter data prefix seen in the assembly: `grTPe_803E90F0` for the callbacks table and `grTPe_803E914C` for the StageData object.
The new header uses an address-based include guard, `GALE01_2228B4`, matching common decomp header-guard style in this repository.
Stage callback fields remain generic `callback0` through `callback3`, with offset comments documenting layout instead of inventing more specific names.
The context-only ground object alias is named `Ground_GObj`, distinguishing ground-specific use from the underlying `HSD_GObj` type.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Converted the assembly `.data` callback array into `StageCallbacks grTPe_803E90F0[4]`, preserving NULL entries and the `0xC0000000` flags as `(1 << 30) | (1 << 31)`.
Converted the stage data block into `StageData grTPe_803E914C`, including the `/GrTPe.dat` string and the callback function pointer order from the assembly.

Matched `grTPeach_80222958` by indexing the callbacks table, calling `Ground_801C14D0(index)`, clearing `Ground` callbacks at `x8`/`xC`, setting the GX link, conditionally installing callback3, invoking callback0, and registering callback2 as an HSD process.
Recreated the OSReport failure path with the string `"%s:%d: couldn t get gobj(id=%d)\n"`, source file `"grtpeach.c"`, line `195`, and the failed index.
Used `Ground_GObj*` signatures for stage callbacks while relying on the normal-build typedef to `HSD_GObj` for ABI compatibility.
Left several no-op and boolean callback functions as minimal C bodies to match `blr`, `li r3,0; blr`, or `li r3,1; blr` assembly patterns.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or formal reviews were present in the PR slice.
The automated report in the PR body lists `src/melee/gr/grtpeach.c` as 100.00% matched, with every listed function at 100.00%.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that reuse `Ground_GObj` or modify the M2CTX definition in `src/melee/gr/forward.h`.
Check whether `tools/convert_data.py`'s `input_glob` was intentionally committed for `grtpeach.s` or later reverted for another data conversion target.
Search for other `grT*` stage module matches that use the same `StageCallbacks`/`StageData` reconstruction pattern.
Look for later renames of `StageCallbacks.callback0` through `callback3` or `Ground` callback fields such as `x8_callback`, `xC_callback`, and `x1C_callback`.
